title: The Midnight Manor Incident
background: Victor Harrington, CEO of Harrington Capital, was found dead in his private study at 3:14am on November 12th, 2026. The door was locked from the inside using a deadbolt. A glass of Scotch sat unfinished on his desk. The medical examiner's report cites blunt force trauma combined with a sedative compound found in his bloodstream. Six guests were staying at Harrington Manor that weekend — all claim to have been asleep.

victim_profile.notes: Victor was about to change his will and complete a controversial merger

timeline.detail: Eleanor, Dominic, Sandra, Marcus, and Priya arrive for the annual retreat.
Heated argument between Victor and Dominic over the firm's upcoming merger. Victor takes a phone call and returns visibly agitated.
Victor tells guests he has important work. Housekeeper confirms bringing him a Scotch at 11:52pm.
CCTV from east wing shows study light extinguishing at 2:02am. No one seen entering after midnight.
Security guard Patrick Holt hears a crash, finds door locked. Breaks it down to find Victor slumped over his desk.

true_motive: Eleanor discovered Victor was about to change his will, removing her as sole heir in favour of a charitable trust. She had also borrowed heavily against her inheritance and faced financial ruin.
true_method: Eleanor dissolved her prescribed Clonazepam into Victor's Scotch to sedate him. During the CCTV gap — caused by a USB loop device she'd planted earlier — she slipped into the study via a duplicate key, struck Victor with the letter opener, then relocked the deadbolt from outside using a thin-blade tool.

suspects.name: Eleanor Harrington
suspects.personality: Quietly controlled. Precise with language. Becomes overly formal when nervous. Has a habit of touching her left wrist when lying.
suspects.alibi: Claims she was asleep in the east guest room all night. Says she heard nothing.
suspects.relationship_to_victim: Younger sister. Sole heir to Victor's estate. Relations had been strained since Victor remarried.
suspects.motive_hint: Stand to inherit everything — unless Victor changed his mind.
secrets: She has a duplicate key to Victor's study made 3 years ago
She borrowed £2.3M against her inheritance and is deeply in debt
She planted a USB loop on the CCTV recorder two days before the weekend
Victor told her that morning he was meeting with his solicitor on Monday

suspects.name: Dominic Foles
suspects.personality: Nervous energy. Quick to deflect. Uses humour to disarm. Goes very still when caught in a lie.
suspects.alibi: Says he went to bed after the argument. Claims he heard Eleanor walking the corridor around 1am.
suspects.relationship_to_victim: Business partner of 15 years. The merger would benefit Dominic enormously — but Victor was getting cold feet.
suspects.motive_hint: The merger he needs falls apart if Victor dies before signing.
secrets: He did go to Victor's study at 11pm to continue the argument — Victor was alive
His fingerprints are on the letter opener because he picked it up during their argument
He is embezzling from the firm and Victor had recently become suspicious

suspects.name: Sandra Rhee
suspects.personality: Composed. Legalistic. Chooses every word with surgical care. Rarely volunteers information.
suspects.alibi: Was in her room all night. Spoke briefly with Marcus Webb in the hallway around 12:30am.
suspects.relationship_to_victim: Victor's attorney for 11 years. Had recently drafted an amendment to his will.
suspects.motive_hint: She knows more about the will than anyone — and she's protecting a secret.
secrets: She drafted the new will that would disinherit Eleanor
She noticed Eleanor entering the study corridor on her way to the bathroom
Victor had asked her to witness the new will on Monday morning

suspects.name: Marcus Webb
suspects.personality: Eager, probing, slightly performative. Talks faster when excited. Knows things he shouldn't.
suspects.alibi: Says he was in the library most of the night, working on an article. Spoke to Sandra around 12:30am.
suspects.relationship_to_victim: Investigating fraud allegations at Harrington Capital. Victor invited him to the manor under the pretence of 'going on the record'.
suspects.motive_hint: A dead source is a ruined story — he needed Victor alive.
secrets: Eleanor called him 3 weeks ago offering him documents on Victor
He had a hidden recorder running throughout the dinner
He saw a figure in the east corridor around 1:15am but assumed it was staff

suspects.name: Priya Nair
suspects.personality: Precise. Scientific. Uncomfortable with imprecision. Gets visibly distressed by false accusations.
suspects.alibi: Was on a video call with her lab team until 1:30am (verifiable). Went directly to sleep.
suspects.relationship_to_victim: Met Victor through mutual investors. He was considering funding her research.
suspects.motive_hint: She's the expert on the compound found in Victor's blood — which makes her a suspect.
secrets: She immediately recognised the sedative compound in the autopsy report as Clonazepam mixed with alcohol
Eleanor had asked her detailed questions about drug interactions two months ago
She has already told this to the police but was asked to stay quiet

evidence.title: Scotch Glass — Lab Report
evidence.description: Traces of Clonazepam detected in the residue. Consistent with a dose sufficient to cause heavy sedation within 30 minutes.
evidence.content:
TOXICOLOGY REPORT — Case #0047
Substance: Clonazepam (benzodiazepine)
Concentration: 8mg equivalent in residue
Effect: Rapid sedation, loss of coordination
Cross-reaction: Dangerous with alcohol
Source: Prescription-grade. Not available OTC.

evidence.title: East Wing Camera — 1:17am Gap
evidence.description: Corridor CCTV shows a 7-minute recording loop between 1:17am and 1:24am. The same 3 seconds repeat on a loop — consistent with a USB playback device planted on the recorder.
evidence.content:
CCTV ANALYSIS REPORT
Camera: East Wing Corridor, Cam-3
Anomaly: Frames 1:17:04–1:24:11 are a repeating loop
Method: USB timestamp override device detected in recorder port
Planted: Estimated 24–48hrs before incident
Technical skill required: LOW — consumer device

evidence.title: Victor's iPhone — Last 24 Hours
evidence.description: Three calls to an unknown number between 10pm–11pm, each under 90 seconds. Text to Sandra Rhee at 10:14pm: 'We need to talk before Monday. The documents.'
evidence.content:
CALL LOG EXTRACT
22:04 — Unknown (+44 7XXX XXXXXX) — 82 sec
22:31 — Unknown (+44 7XXX XXXXXX) — 45 sec
22:58 — Unknown (+44 7XXX XXXXXX) — 71 sec

SMS to Sandra Rhee 22:14:
'We need to talk before Monday. The documents.'

evidence.title: Burned Document Fragment
evidence.description: Ash recovered from the study fireplace. Partially reconstructed: a legal transfer document. The beneficiary name is burned away but the solicitor's stamp (Rhee & Associates) is visible.
evidence.content:
PARTIALLY RECONSTRUCTED DOCUMENT
[HARRINGTON ESTATE — AMENDMENT TO WILL]
...hereby revoke all previous provisions regarding...
...transfer of ALL voting shares and liquid assets to...
[NAME ILLEGIBLE — burned]
...effective upon signing and witnessing by...
Stamp: RHEE & ASSOCIATES — LONDON

evidence.title: Prescription Bottle — East Bookshelf
evidence.description: A small amber bottle found behind the east bookshelf. Prescribed to Eleanor Harrington. Drug: Clonazepam 2mg. 11 tablets remain from a prescription of 30 — 7 tablets unaccounted for beyond normal dosage schedule.
evidence.content:
EVIDENCE TAG: EA-007
Item: Prescription bottle — Clonazepam 2mg
Prescribed to: Eleanor R. Harrington
Issued: 28 October 2026 (30 tablets)
Remaining: 11 tablets
Location found: Behind east bookshelf, Victor's study
Note: Normal usage over 2 weeks = ~12 tablets. 7 unaccounted.

evidence.title: Letter Opener — Fingerprint Report
evidence.description: Two sets of prints on the letter opener found under Victor's chair. Set A: Victor's. Set B: Dominic Foles. Dominic's prints are fresh (under 12 hours old) and he claims he never entered the study.
evidence.content:
FINGERPRINT ANALYSIS — Item LO-01
Print Set A: Victor J. Harrington — CONFIRMED
Print Set B: Dominic A. Foles — CONFIRMED
Age of Set B prints: 6–12 hours (fresh)
Force indication: Gripped firmly, consistent with use as weapon
Blood trace: Micro-droplet on handle, Type O — matches victim

evidence.title: Study Floor — Crime Scene Photos
evidence.description: High-resolution photos reveal scuff marks near the east bookshelf consistent with a hidden door mechanism. A thin rectangular impression in the dust suggests an object was recently removed.
evidence.content:
CRIME SCENE PHOTOGRAPHY — Study Floor
Note A: Scuff marks (30cm × 2cm) near east bookshelf base
Note B: Rectangular dust impression (28cm × 4cm) — object removed recently
Note C: Victor's chair slightly displaced from usual position
Note D: No signs of forced entry on main door

evidence.title: Harrington Capital — Merger Documents
evidence.description: Draft merger agreement showing Dominic Foles stood to receive a £14M payout upon completion. Victor had handwritten 'PAUSE — review fraud exposure' in the margin.
evidence.content:
HARRINGTON CAPITAL — MERGER TERM SHEET v4.2
Party A: Harrington Capital
Party B: Nexus Private Equity
Dominic Foles executive incentive: £14,200,000 upon close
Victor handwritten note: 'PAUSE — review fraud exposure'
Date of note: Nov 11, 2026 (day of death)

evidence.title: Duplicate Key Receipt — Locksmiths Record
evidence.description: A locksmith receipt from 2023 shows a duplicate key was cut for the study deadbolt. The customer name has been scratched off the paper receipt but the phone number matches Eleanor Harrington's mobile.
evidence.content:
LOCKSMITH RECORD — Cotswold Key Cutters
Date: 14 March 2023
Item: Deadbolt duplicate — Harrington Manor study
Customer phone: +44 7XXX XXXXXX [MATCHES ELEANOR]
Customer name: [SCRATCHED OUT]
Note: Paid cash
